questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the resistance of a filament lamp
questions.question: A learner uses a filament lamp graph to check the key physics idea. What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases?
options: Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
Its resistance changes only if the potential difference is zero.
Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.
questions.senNote: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the resistance of a filament lamp
questions.question: A learner uses a component test circuit to check the key physics idea. What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases?
options: Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
The current stops because the filament emits light.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.
questions.senNote: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the resistance of a filament lamp
questions.question: A learner uses a I-V practical result to check the key physics idea. What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases?
options: Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
The lamp becomes a perfect conductor at high temperature.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.
questions.senNote: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the resistance of a filament lamp
questions.question: A learner uses a current sensor trace to check the key physics idea. What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases?
options: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
The resistance is removed by the power supply.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.
questions.senNote: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the resistance of a filament lamp
questions.question: A learner uses a filament lamp graph to prepare for an exam-style question. What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases? Choose the best answer.
options: Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
The filament cools as current increases.
Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.
questions.senNote: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the resistance of a filament lamp
questions.question: A learner uses a component test circuit to prepare for an exam-style question. What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases? Choose the best answer.
options: Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
The graph must be a straight line through the origin.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.
questions.senNote: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the resistance of a filament lamp
questions.question: A learner uses a I-V practical result to prepare for an exam-style question. What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases?
options: Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
The resistance becomes negative at high current.
Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.
questions.senNote: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe the resistance of a filament lamp
questions.question: A learner uses a current sensor trace to prepare for an exam-style question. What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases?
options: Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
The potential difference is used up inside the filament.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.
questions.senNote: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain the purpose of a fuse
questions.question: A learner uses a three-pin plug diagram to check the key physics idea. Why is a fuse placed in the live wire of a plug?
options: It keeps the neutral wire at a higher voltage.
It increases the current to start the appliance.
It stores charge so the appliance works longer.
It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
questions.correctAnswer: It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.
questions.senNote: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain the purpose of a fuse
questions.question: A learner uses a home wiring safety check to check the key physics idea. Why is a fuse placed in the live wire of a plug?
options: It keeps the neutral wire at a higher voltage.
It increases the current to start the appliance.
It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
It protects the appliance by making the earth wire live.
questions.correctAnswer: It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.
questions.senNote: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain the purpose of a fuse
questions.question: A learner uses a fuse-rating explanation to check the key physics idea. Why is a fuse placed in the live wire of a plug?
options: It keeps the neutral wire at a higher voltage.
It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
It increases the current to start the appliance.
It makes the cable hotter so it melts safely.
questions.correctAnswer: It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.
questions.senNote: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain the purpose of a fuse
questions.question: A learner uses a appliance fault scenario to check the key physics idea. Why is a fuse placed in the live wire of a plug?
options: It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
It keeps the neutral wire at a higher voltage.
It increases the current to start the appliance.
It stops the plug from using any energy.
questions.correctAnswer: It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.
questions.senNote: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain the purpose of a fuse
questions.question: A learner uses a three-pin plug diagram to prepare for an exam-style question. Why is a fuse placed in the live wire of a plug? Choose the best answer.
options: It keeps the neutral wire at a higher voltage.
It increases the current to start the appliance.
It is fitted so the neutral wire breaks first.
It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
questions.correctAnswer: It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.
questions.senNote: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain the purpose of a fuse
questions.question: A learner uses a home wiring safety check to prepare for an exam-style question. Why is a fuse placed in the live wire of a plug? Choose the best answer.
options: It keeps the neutral wire at a higher voltage.
It increases the current to start the appliance.
It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
It reduces the supply voltage to zero at all times.
questions.correctAnswer: It melts and breaks the circuit if the current becomes too large.
questions.explanation: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.
questions.senNote: A fuse is a safety device. It melts if the current is too large and cuts off the supply.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Calculate the cost of electricity
questions.question: A learner uses a household energy bill to check the key physics idea. A 2 kW heater runs for 3 hours. Electricity costs 25 p per kWh. What is the cost?
questions.explanation: Cost equals power in kW multiplied by time in hours multiplied by the unit rate.
questions.senNote: Cost equals power in kW multiplied by time in hours multiplied by the unit rate.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Calculate the cost of electricity
questions.question: A learner uses a portable heater use case to check the key physics idea. A 3 kW heater runs for 4 hours. Electricity costs 26 p per kWh. What is the cost?
questions.explanation: Cost equals power in kW multiplied by time in hours multiplied by the unit rate.
questions.senNote: Cost equals power in kW multiplied by time in hours multiplied by the unit rate.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Calculate the cost of electricity
questions.question: A learner uses a kitchen appliance meter reading to check the key physics idea. A 1 kW heater runs for 5 hours. Electricity costs 27 p per kWh. What is the cost?
questions.explanation: Cost equals power in kW multiplied by time in hours multiplied by the unit rate.
questions.senNote: Cost equals power in kW multiplied by time in hours multiplied by the unit rate.

questions.explanation: Cost equals power in kW multiplied by time in hours multiplied by the unit rate.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Use power = potential difference x current
questions.question: A learner uses a phone charger label to check the key physics idea. An appliance works at 14 V and 2 A. What is its power?
options: 7 W
questions.explanation: Electrical power equals potential difference multiplied by current.
questions.senNote: Electrical power equals potential difference multiplied by current.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a parallel lamp circuit to check the key physics idea. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct?
options: The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
The voltage is shared equally only in a series circuit.
The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a two-branch circuit diagram to check the key physics idea. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct?
options: The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
Each branch receives half the supply voltage automatically.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a potential difference measurement to check the key physics idea. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct?
options: The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
Components in different branches must have the same current.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a circuit board check to check the key physics idea. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct?
options: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
Opening one branch always switches every branch off.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a parallel lamp circuit to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct? Choose the best answer.
options: The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
The battery current is zero when branches are added.
The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a two-branch circuit diagram to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct? Choose the best answer.
options: The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
The potential difference only exists before the junction.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a potential difference measurement to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct? Choose the best answer.
options: The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
The cells share charge equally between electrons.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a circuit board check to prepare for an exam-style question. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct?
options: The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
The voltage across a branch depends only on wire length.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall potential difference in a parallel circuit
questions.question: A learner uses a parallel lamp circuit to test a common misconception. Which statement about potential difference in a parallel circuit is correct?
options: The supply voltage becomes zero in each branch.
The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
The current is the same in each branch because the voltage changes.
Every branch must contain identical components.
questions.correctAnswer: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
questions.explanation: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.
questions.senNote: In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full supply potential difference.

options: The potential difference across each branch is the same as the supply.
